titlestmt.titleproper: Dorothy Whitney Straight Elmhirst papers from Dartington Hall3782
titlestmt.author: Compiled by {Name}

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-03-13 18:16:21 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Dorothy Whitney Straight Elmhirst papers from Dartington Hall
did.abstract: Includes photocopies of correspondence with Margaret Isherwood, Eloise Elmhirst Sharman, Ellen Van Volkenberg Browne and correspondence concerning Willard Straight Hall; lecture notes, notebooks, diaries and travel journals; an inventory for the records room at Dartington Hall and a diary describing World War I by an unknown author. Also, microfilmed correspondence between Leonard K. Elmhirst and Dorothy Whitney Straight; her diaries, clippings, social appointments, notes and addresses.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Philanthropist, educator, publisher, patron of the arts and social activist.

Dorothy Payne Whitney, daughter of William C. Whitney, a financier and Secretary of the Navy under President Grover Cleveland, married Willard Dickerman Straight in 19ll. Straight died in 1918, and she married Leonard Knight Elmhirst in 1925. Dorothy and Elmhirst purchased Dartington Hall, Devon, England, reconstructed the 14th century manor and founded a school on the property. Dartington Hall became a center for the arts and a leading coeducational progressive school. The New School for Social Research in New York was begun with the backing of the Whitney Estate in 1919. Dorothy was involved with the New York State Food Commission, the Women's Liberty Loan Committee, the Social Unit Plan, the Junior League, the War Camp Service Committee, the Women's Emergency Committee of the European Relief Council, the New School for Social Research, the Tuberculosis Preventorium for Children, the Pueblos Defense Committee, and the Mayor's Committee of Women on National Defense. She supported individual students at Bordentown Military Academy and Hobart College, and helped to found THE NEW REPUBLIC.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopies of correspondence with Margaret Isherwood, Eloise Elmhirst Sharman, Ellen Van Volkenberg Browne and correspondence concerning Willard Straight Hall (1920-1967); also lecture notes, notes about lectures taught at Dartington Hall, notebooks, diaries and travel journals; contains originals of letters by Ruth Morgan (1926-1933), Margaret and Helen Isherwood (1932-1968), Susan Sedgwick Hammond (1940-1968), Louise E. Croly (1926-1940) and letters by Dorothy and Leonard to Margaret and Helen Isherwood, Nancy Green, Ellen Van Volkenberg Browne, and Gilbert Harrison; also included are notes by E.A. Stettner, the transcriber of the Croly correspondence, an inventory for the records room at Dartington Hall and a diary (n.d.) describing World War I by an unknown author. Also, microfilmed correspondence between Leonard K. Elmhirst and Dorothy Whitney Straight (1920-1925) discussing travels, love and their subsequent marriage; correspondence (1932-1935) discussing psychology, sociology, Dartington Hall, education, religion and literature between Henry F. Heard, Gerald Heard, Margaret Isherwood and Dorothy Whitney Straight Elmhirst; her diaries (1927-1928), clippings, social appointments, notes and addresses. Also, clippings concerning events at Dartington Hall (ca.1926-1949), and photographs of Dorothy Whitney Straight Elmhirst's penthouse on Park Avenue (ca.1928). Also an inventory catalog of the Dartington Hall Archives.

relatedmaterial.head: NOTES
relatedmaterial.p: Related collection:Dorothy Whitney Straight Elmhirst. Papers, #3725.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Dorothy Whitney Straight Elmhirst papers from Dartington Hall, #3782. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the printed finding aid

did.unittitle: Dorothy Whitney Straight Elmhirst notes on religion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also dates from 1959 to 1968 and undated notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding Gerald Heard and Margaret Isherwood along with clippings regarding religion, some of which is undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on "The Possessed" and articles of interest.

did.unittitle: Notes on Blake's Eternity, et al.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on "Ring Around the Moon," "Hamlet" and English furniture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook on "Books as I have Read Them"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks on lectures, and on quotations from Pueblo Indian book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on English furniture, on the "Tempest", etc. circa 1950's.

did.unittitle: "Something of What I have Learned in this Year of Grace"
DWSE Extracts and Notes for a Lecture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Quotes and notes on Shakespeare's sonnets and extracts from Neumann's study of Henry Moore and Bradley on Lear.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of DWSE's diaries, clippings, social appointments, notes and addresses. Positive film only.
